issue.title: What does this issue mean?
issue.summary:
Geopolitical Escalation Supply Chain Demo Case

A fictional regional security update reports rising geopolitical tension near a major maritime corridor used by container ships, energy cargoes, and industrial components. Several shipping firms say they are reviewing routing options, insurance coverage, crew safety procedures, and contingency plans for customers with time-sensitive deliveries. Manufacturers that depend on imported components are asking logistics teams to estimate where delays coul...
issue.core_issue:
Geopolitical Escalation Supply Chain Demo Case

A fictional regional security update reports rising geopolitical tension near a major maritime corridor used by container ships, energy cargoes, and industrial components.
uncertainties: Instead, it describes uncertainty around route reliability, insurance pricing, port congestion, and the cost of rerouting cargo.
Executives want a structured briefing that separates confirmed operational changes from issues that still require monitoring.

It is designed to test whether the Strategic Intelligence Agent can identify a geopolitical escalation and supply chain disruption, detect mechanisms such as routing uncertainty and supply chain reconfiguration, retrieve historical analogue patterns, and generate decision-support questions without predicting whether the situation will worsen.
evidence:
Geopolitical Escalation Supply Chain Demo Case

A fictional regional security update reports rising geopolitical tension near a major maritime corridor used by container ships, energy cargoes, and industrial components. Several shipping firms say they are reviewing routing options, insurance coverage, crew safety procedures, and contingency plans for customers with time-sensitive deliveries. Manufacturers that depend on imported components are asking logistics teams to estimate where delays coul...

strategic_assessment.direct_answer: The strongest historical similarity is a Supply chain disruption event affecting automotive, shipping. Across relevant historical cases, organizations most often reviewed supplier concentration or diversified supply options; reviewed continuity plans and operational exposure. The key pattern is that operational adaptation usually follows the first policy or market shock. The main risk is assuming the headline event is the whole story; the most important uncertainty is how rules, customers, suppliers, and management responses change after implementation. The next signal to watch is concrete exposure mapping or policy guidance. The comparison guardrail is: Compare with logistics, supplier concentration, chokepoint, and continuity-planning cases.
strategic_assessment.comparison_guardrail: Compare with logistics, supplier concentration, chokepoint, and continuity-planning cases.

historical_patterns.pattern: Reviewed supplier concentration or diversified supply options
historical_patterns.interpretation: Similar cases often pushed organizations to reduce dependence on a narrow set of suppliers, routes, or inputs.

historical_patterns.pattern: Reviewed continuity plans and operational exposure
historical_patterns.interpretation: Operationally exposed cases often led teams to review contingency plans, routes, and concentration risks.

historical_patterns.pattern: Increased executive communication and guidance monitoring
historical_patterns.interpretation: Uncertain events often made management communication and stakeholder updates more important.

outcome_distribution.interpretation: Cases in this group show meaningful constraints, delays, or access limits that required operational response.
Cases in this group show organizations adjusting processes, monitoring, or planning rather than stopping operations entirely.

strategic_watchlist: Regulatory clarification, implementation dates, exemptions, or licensing guidance.
Management commentary about customer exposure, supplier exposure, or operational constraints.
Supplier diversification announcements, qualification updates, or inventory adjustments.
Capex, localization, or sourcing changes tied to the event.
Evidence that affected organizations are revising contracts, compliance workflows, or customer terms.

investor_view: Monitor revenue exposure, margin pressure, management guidance, and customer concentration commentary.
Separate operational disruption signals from ordinary quarterly volatility.
Watch whether management distinguishes temporary disruption from durable business-model exposure.
corporate_strategy_view: Monitor localization requirements, policy implementation details, supplier concentration, and eligibility rules.
Identify which business units need updated exposure maps or decision memos.
Prepare executive discussion notes that separate known facts from unresolved questions.
supply_chain_view: Monitor inventory coverage, logistics routes, alternative suppliers, and qualification timelines.
Check whether single-source inputs or chokepoints require contingency planning.
Track customer communication needs if delivery timing or input availability changes.
policy_view: Monitor implementation guidance, enforcement posture, exemptions, and timing.
Separate announced policy intent from operational rules that organizations can actually follow.
Track whether agencies, regulators, or counterpart governments clarify scope or eligibility.

important_limitations: Educational use only; this is a local decision-support demo, not a production advisory service.
This is not investment advice, trading advice, legal advice, or a forecast.
Human review is recommended before operational, legal, financial, or executive use.
Historical analogues help structure comparison but do not predict future outcomes.
Outcome frequencies are counts within the local curated dataset, not real-world probabilities.
The system does not use live web retrieval or source verification.

evidence_credibility.evidence_summary: 5 historical outcomes were retrieved. 3 are high confidence and 2 are medium confidence. Source URLs are not fabricated; source status is reported separately. 3 rule-based strategic lesson(s) were generated from those outcomes.
key_limitations: Historical outcomes are simplified educational summaries.
Confidence reflects internal evidence coding, not real-world predictive accuracy.
Lessons are pattern-based and should be reviewed by a human analyst.
Source URLs are not fabricated; missing source links remain marked as source pending.
evidence_credibility.reviewer_note: Use these outcomes and lessons as decision-support context. They do not prove factual, legal, geopolitical, financial, or predictive accuracy.

decision_case.situation:
Geopolitical Escalation Supply Chain Demo Case

A fictional regional security update reports rising geopolitical tension near a major maritime corridor used by container ships, energy cargoes, and industrial components.
decision_options: Option A: wait for more complete information before changing posture.
Option B: map exposure, preserve flexibility, and prepare staged response options.
Option C: move immediately to a more defensive posture.
assumptions: The supplied material is sufficient for an initial decision-support brief.
Human review will verify source details before operational use.
The Supply Chain Disruption frame is an appropriate starting point for analysis.
The identified mechanisms remain relevant to the decision context: Operational Resilience, Security Escalation, Information Uncertainty.
Sector exposure should be validated against organization-specific data.
risks: The source material may omit operational details needed for final action.
Historical similarity may not transfer cleanly to the current context.
New evidence may change the preferred path.
The Supply Chain Disruption classification may be incomplete if the input is brief or ambiguous.
decision_case.recommended_path: Option B: map exposure, preserve flexibility, and prepare staged response options.
change_triggers: New primary source material changes the factual basis.
Stakeholder responses differ from the assumptions in the brief.
Implementation details create materially different constraints.
Evidence shows the situation no longer fits the Supply Chain Disruption frame.
limitations: Educational use only; this is a local decision-support demo, not a production advisory service.
This is not investment advice, trading advice, legal advice, or a forecast.
Human review is recommended before operational, legal, financial, or executive use.
Historical analogues help structure comparison but do not predict future outcomes.
Outcome frequencies are counts within the local curated dataset, not real-world probabilities.
The system does not use live web retrieval or source verification.

The system does not use live web retrieval or source verification.
confidence_assessment.rationale: Moderate confidence because 11 reviewable evidence items were recorded, historical analogues are available, limitations remain visible.

direct_answer_quality.label: Strong
direct_answer_quality.rationale: Checks whether the output frames a decision question, situation, recommendation, and criteria.

historical_analogue_relevance.label: Strong
historical_analogue_relevance.rationale: Checks whether analogues exist, appear in the evidence ledger, and include transferability limits.

evidence_use.label: Strong
evidence_use.rationale: Checks whether evidence items are reviewable and uncertainty remains visible.

option_clarity.label: Strong
option_clarity.rationale: Checks whether options are distinct and a preferred path is identified.

risk_identification.label: Strong
risk_identification.rationale: Checks whether risks, limitations, and unknowns are explicit.

change_trigger_quality.label: Strong
change_trigger_quality.rationale: Checks whether the recommendation includes observable change triggers and monitoring signals.

localization_quality.label: Strong
localization_quality.rationale: English output selected; localization transformation is not required.

overconfidence_control.label: Adequate
overconfidence_control.rationale: Checks for qualitative confidence, visible limitations, and absence of overconfident phrases.

notes: Scores are internal review aids, not real-world accuracy claims.
No LLM evaluator, network access, or external benchmark is used.

trace.detail: IssueExtractor: Every route starts by converting source text into structured issue fields.
ScenarioClassifier: Scenario classification is required before retrieval decisions can be interpreted.
HistoricalRetriever: Supply Chain Disruption benefits from comparison against historical precedents.
ContextRetriever: Supply Chain Disruption usually benefits from domain context and monitoring considerations.
ImplicationAnalyzer: Selected retrieval outputs need to be synthesized into analyst-facing considerations.
BriefGenerator: The final deliverable is an executive intelligence brief.

reasoning_record.why: Every route starts by converting source text into structured issue fields.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Core issue, actors, industries, policy terms, and document type.

reasoning_record.why: Scenario classification is required before retrieval decisions can be interpreted.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Primary scenario and matched keyword evidence.

reasoning_record.why: Supply Chain Disruption benefits from comparison against historical precedents.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Top analogue cases and similarity reasons from the historical database.

reasoning_record.why: Supply Chain Disruption usually benefits from domain context and monitoring considerations.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Adds current context from the local knowledge base.

reasoning_record.why: Selected retrieval outputs need to be synthesized into analyst-facing considerations.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Similarities, differences, business considerations, operational considerations, geopolitical considerations, and questions.

reasoning_record.why: The final deliverable is an executive intelligence brief.
reasoning_record.expected_contribution: Markdown brief with evidence sources, trace, and analysis path.

evaluation_metadata.note: Run artifacts are generated by the local deterministic pipeline; they are not real-world accuracy claims.
